title: scenarios 57-68: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)
description:
Brief 003 (lead-0h1) — Event-driven shop activation. 12 scenarios extending the
shop-templates bootstrap surface to ship a canonical `.claude/settings.json`
per shop type plus host-prereq naming, and to install sibling BC clones
editable from the lead bootstrap.

The brief carries two invariants and five scope items:

- Invariant 1: every shop is reactive on session start (BC watches own
  `inbox/`; lead watches `repos/*/outbox/`).
- Invariant 2: activation honors brief 001's encapsulation — the
  watcher fires a wake signal; reading content still goes through
  `shop-msg`.
- Scope A: canonical `.claude/settings.json` per shop type (scenarios 57, 68).
- Scope B: SessionStart hook content with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait,
  alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged 2>&1 for startup-chatter
  visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming (scenarios 58, 59, 65).
- Scope C: bootstrap writes settings.json; update re-pours it as a
  bootstrap-managed file (scenarios 60, 61, 62).
- Scope D: prereq naming (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`,
  `stdbuf`) in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` and the canonical primer
  (scenarios 63, 64).
- Scope E: live-source resolution outcome + lead bootstrap installs
  sibling BC clones editable into the product venv (scenarios 66, 67).

## Empirically validated context

This session validated the stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipeline end-to-end
on lead-79q reconciliation: sub-second event-fire on `work_done`
arrival via the reference pipeline named in scope item B. The pattern
works. Scope items A/B/C/D codify the canonical shape so every
bootstrapped shop gets it by default.

## Pre-state cited

Pre-state verification on shopsystem-templates current state:

- Package data at `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files
  (`bc-implementer.md`, `bc-reviewer.md`, `lead-po.md`, `lead-architect.md`)
  + 2 CLAUDE.md primers under `templates/claude/` (`bc.md`, `lead.md`) +
  `gitignore.template`. No `.claude/settings.json` template exists.
- `src/shop_templates/cli.py`: bootstrap writes `.claude/agents/*.md`,
  top-level `CLAUDE.md` (from primer + `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution),
  top-level `.gitignore`, and invokes `bd init --skip-agents`. No
  `.claude/settings.json` is written. `update` re-pours managed
  agent files only — no settings.json handling.
- Empirical bootstrap of a probe directory
  (`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe
  --target /tmp/probe-003`) produced exactly: `CLAUDE.md`,
  `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md`, `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`.
  No `.claude/settings.json`. Exit 0.
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` scan: no existing scenarios
  pin SessionStart hooks, settings.json content, Monitor harness,
  inotifywait, or venv install mode.

All 12 scenarios target net-new capability — `assign_scenarios` is the
correct vehicle.

## Implementation notes (informational; bodies are the contract)

- Scope item E is realization-pinned by the PO via two scenarios:
  scenario 66 pins the outcome (live-source resolution on session
  start) and scenario 67 pins the install-shape (lead bootstrap
  installs sibling clones editable into the product venv). Scenario 67
  alone is the PO's leaning shape; the Implementer is free to add a
  hook-time refresh on top if pre-state on E surfaces drift cases not
  covered by 67 alone, or to treat 67 as sufficient.

- Scenario 65 pins the "loud fail on missing `inotifywait`" property.
  The Implementer must select a hook shape such that a host with no
  `inotifywait` on PATH surfaces a visible diagnostic — not a silent
  no-watcher state. A polling fallback that silently activates
  violates the invariant; if the Implementer commits a fallback, it
  must itself be a visible notification.

- Scenario 59 specifies the canonical lead variant **composes** with the
  existing `bd prime` `SessionStart` hook — two distinct entries under
  `SessionStart`, not a replacement.

- Scope D's prereq naming surface is the Implementer's call between
  `CLAUDE.md` primer (named in scenarios 63 + 64 as the realization)
  vs other places. The scenarios commit primer + bootstrap-generated
  CLAUDE.md as the surface; the Implementer must satisfy both.

## Procedural reminders

- Hash discipline: per `lead-wgv`, verify computed hashes against
  wire form, not against the body source pre-CLI. The `shop-msg send`
  CLI computes hashes from canonicalized wire form; the Implementer's
  BDD harness must read from the same wire form so verification holds.

- Mailbox encapsulation: `shop-msg` is the only sanctioned mailbox
  boundary on both sides. Do not hand-edit mailbox YAML; do not `cat`
  it. If `shop-msg pending` fails on a stale venv, fix the install —
  do not bypass the CLI (per `lead-xq0`).

- Bead ID: `lead-xkh` is the lead-shop work_id for this assignment.
  No dotted suffix; non-dotted lead-shop bead IDs per `lead-4wy`.

title: brief-003: event-driven shop activation via SessionStart-armed Monitor (inotifywait)
description: PO draft brief 003. Bake an inotifywait-based Monitor activation pattern into the canonical .claude/settings.json shipped via shopsystem-templates, propagated through brief 002's bootstrap surface. Two invariants: (1) every shop is reactive on session start (no polling required); (2) activation honors brief 001 encapsulation (event fires the wake; shop-msg CLI remains sole content surface). Scope items A-E: A canonical settings.json per shop type; B SessionStart hook prompt content (validated stdbuf+inotifywait pipeline); C bootstrap writes settings.json; D prereqs documentation; E venv install drift mitigation (folds in lead-xq0). Sequencing: dispatch may proceed immediately on close. Target BC: shopsystem-templates. Vehicle hint: assign_scenarios.
notes:
Clarify response on lead-xkh (BC: shopsystem-templates): BC's Reviewer caught contradiction between scenario 60 (c8002527857e0dd1, NEW in lead-xkh — bootstrap writes .claude/settings.json) and scenario 51 (7d64bb8ed5a3f656, prior in lead-2gr / brief 002 — bootstrap leaves no .claude/settings.json).

Resolution (user-driver, router call per autonomous-run): retire scenario 51's .claude/settings.json clause; keep its no-top-level-AGENTS.md clause. Brief 003 (this bead) explicitly commits bootstrap to writing .claude/settings.json — scenario 60 carries the positive pin going forward.

Authored scenario 69 at features/templates/69-bootstrap-leaves-no-top-level-agents-md.gherkin pinning only the surviving "no top-level AGENTS.md" property. Supersedes relationship made explicit via Feature: description block at top of file (no existing supersedes-tag convention found in features/templates/ or in any of the BC's feature files; description-block approach establishes a readable in-file convention for now).

Vehicle observation from BC carried forward to Architect: BC's Reviewer suggests request_bugfix more accurate than assign_scenarios for the brief 003 dispatch since BC already carries bootstrap pins. Architect's call on dispatching scenario 69 retirement.

Next step: Architect dispatches request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates carrying scenario 69, with dispatch description instructing Implementer to retire scenario 51 (hash 7d64bb8ed5a3f656) from repos/shopsystem-templates/features/bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature when landing 69.

title: [bug] Bootstrap must invoke bd init --skip-agents and primers must reference bd discipline (lead-k8v §4.4 round 2)
description: Closure for lead-k8v clarify (brief 002 §4.4 round). The bootstrap dispatch's 20 scenarios (lead-k8v) didn't pin bd init's side effects: AGENTS.md, .claude/settings.json, beads-block append to CLAUDE.md. User-driver resolution: bootstrap invokes bd init --skip-agents (suppresses all three primary side effects per empirical verification against bd 1.0.3); shopsystem-templates canonical CLAUDE.md primers carry bd-discipline references (bd prime) directly. PO authored 4 additive scenarios (50-53). Dispatch as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates. Terminal work_done on this work_id implicitly closes lead-k8v's clarify chain. Round 2 of brief 002 §4.4 closure (round 1 = lead-k8v's 20 scenarios).
close_reason: work_done complete from shopsystem-templates verified: 4 dispatched hashes (32d99f6d4a2dad37, 7d64bb8ed5a3f656, 5ec07c275350ba81, 2afb5cba3ea3de25) match; prior pin 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e echoed back regression-free; all 4 mutation-verified. Post-state spot-check: cli.py _bd_init_in invokes subprocess [bd, init, --skip-agents] (line 192); both primers templates/claude/{bc,lead}.md contain 'bd prime' substring with bd-discipline blocks. Empirical /tmp bootstrap probe (shop-type bc): AGENTS.md absent, .claude/settings.json absent, CLAUDE.md present with 'bd prime', .beads/ initialized. Brief 002 round-2 §4.4 closure landed.

title: shopsystem-templates: assign brief-002 bootstrap CLI scenarios (items A-E)
notes:
2026-05-13 (Architect): assign_scenarios dispatched to shopsystem-templates.

Pre-state (empirical):
- `shop-templates --help` → only {list, show}. `init`/`update`/`bootstrap` all rejected with exit 2 "invalid choice".
- Package data `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files only (bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md, lead-architect.md, lead-po.md). No CLAUDE.md primer template. No shop-type discriminator anywhere in cli.py.
- Lead-shop and BC-router CLAUDE.md surfaces (post-user-edits, post-brief-001) verified read-able for elevation source.

Q1 (capability exists?) → NO (verified). All 20 scenarios are net-new behavior; vehicle = assign_scenarios. Q2/Q3 N/A.

Item E PDR-trigger decision: brief-level dispatch, no PDR.
- Two CLAUDE.md surfaces now contain mostly router-primer content (post-brief-001 discipline); per-shop intrusions are narrow (shop name, sibling BC list for lead, BC product description for BC).
- Brief committed CLAUDE.md as init-only (managed/shop-owned tension dissolves after init).
- Scenario 44 (canonical primer template ships) and 45 (generated file names shop identity + role set) commit substitution intent without naming syntax — Implementer's call.
- No need to split CLAUDE.md into managed/shop-owned halves.

Bundling: single assign_scenarios. All 20 scenarios share one product surface (shop-templates CLI extension + package data + bootstrap behavior). PO stance item 4 committed E to ship with A-D in one dispatch; Implementer sequences elevation→consumption internally.

Dispatch:
- Vehicle: assign_scenarios
- BC: shopsystem-templates
- work_id: lead-k8v
- feature-title: "shop-templates bootstrap CLI surface (brief 002, items A-E)"
- bc-tag: shopsystem-templates
- 20 scenarios (features/templates/26-*.gherkin through 45-*.gherkin)
- Exit: 0
- Verified deposited via `shop-msg pending inbox` (shows "lead-k8v assign_scenarios") and `shop-msg read inbox --work-id lead-k8v` (20 hashes, all CLI-tagged).

Scenario hashes (in dispatch order):
26: 0f50666b7e86f1ba   27: 8893150c874eb179   28: d6e935bca62d0039   29: 7941cdc591cd2c3b
30: 6830d0f1100055a0   31: b1bd59e48496d438   32: 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e   33: 6b0006b5fc93b637
34: 69496ea1e5c82ed3   35: b8dd3bbc45674f90   36: 32d21a2277dbbc94   37: 264322ae65312bc7
38: 03b4e3fa31d72031   39: 56a0ac7107ba5c15   40: ca0f0a249d025267   41: 3f4d7d2256a97ae7
42: efae77e534588357   43: 575c0f31c5e6e8e6   44: 0cce58eb573d3c91   45: a15dac2f87549b8a

Procedural notes for BC Implementer (recorded here in case clarify references work_id):
- Hash discipline (per lead-5cv precedent, lead-4qy finding, lead-wgv canonicalization ambiguity): verify each scenario_hash against the WIRE form via `shop-msg read inbox`, NOT against the raw scenario body. The lead CLI hashed the sentinel-tagged form before tag substitution; wire form is what the schema validates and what the BC must reproduce.
- Item E elevation source: CURRENT content of /workspaces/shopsystem-product/CLAUDE.md (lead-shop primer, post-user-edits + post-brief-001) and /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md (BC-router primer, post-brief-001 + post-user-edits). Substitution syntax is Implementer's call within scenarios 44+45 invariants.
- Target dir for canonical templates: src/shop_templates/templates/. Extension naming convention: TBD by Implementer (existing role prompts use `.md`).
close_reason: Brief 002 §4.4 closure chain closed: round 1 (20 scenarios for items A-E) + round 2 (lead-2gr's 4 scenarios closing bd init side-effect gap) both terminal-complete. lead-2gr work_done verified and closed; its closure transitively closes lead-k8v's clarify chain. Post-state in shopsystem-templates carries the full bootstrap+update surface, --skip-agents discipline, and bd-primer references.

title: shopsystem-templates: assign brief-002 bootstrap CLI scenarios (items A-E)
close_reason: dotted ID; clarify-time pattern incompatibility per lead-4wy; reissuing with non-dotted ID

title: request_bugfix: pin parallel per-step shop-msg read CLI naming in lead-po/bc-implementer/bc-reviewer subsections (round 3 of §4.4 closure on lead-bwu)
description:
Round 3 §4.4 closure for brief 001 items B+D. Parent chain: lead-231 (brief 001) -> lead-bwu (initial 9 scenarios) -> lead-e4g (scenario 46, lead-architect parallel) -> THIS BEAD.

Trigger: lead-e4g clarify reported asymmetry: Implementer extended fix to all four canonical templates (lead-po, lead-architect, bc-implementer, bc-reviewer) but scenario coverage only pinned lead-architect (scenario 46). The three sister subsections need parallel per-step pinning.

Carries scenarios 47, 48, 49 (one each for lead-po H3 'Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI', bc-implementer H2 'Your job', bc-reviewer H2 'What you read'). All three mirror scenario 46's three-Then structure (positive per-step CLI naming + bare-action-verbs regression + path-shape regression).

PO authored, Architect dispatched via shop-msg send request_bugfix. Implementer's terminal work_done on this bead closes lead-bwu and lead-e4g together.
close_reason: BC work_done complete; 3 hashes match dispatch exactly (14837e4ef2ea004d, 4671ccec78520c83, 688a8f10fe2c5a2f). Post-state spot-check: features/*_cli_naming.feature landed; lead-po line 160, bc-implementer line 28, bc-reviewer line 17, lead-architect line 272 all name shop-msg read inbox|outbox per-step. Inbox-flavored step defs appended to tests/conftest.py; 70 BDD passed. Reviewer probes: bare-verb + path-shape corruption regressions failed-as-expected and were restored. No src/ changes (pre-state already conformed from lead-e4g).

title: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: scenario 46 closes lead-bwu clarify (per-step shop-msg read outbox in lead-architect 'Responding to BC clarify' subsection)
description:
Closure dispatch for the §4.4 clarify loop opened by lead-bwu.

Parent: lead-bwu (request_bugfix carrying 9 scenarios for brief lead-231 items B+D). Grandparent: lead-231 (brief 001).

Trigger: lead-bwu's clarify (BC Implementer/Reviewer in shopsystem-templates) flagged an asymmetry between lead-architect.md line 272 (bare 'Read the clarify' opening step in 'Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' subsection — no shop-msg read outbox cite on the same step) and lead-po.md lines 160-162 (explicit 'Read the BC's clarify via shop-msg read outbox --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>' form). Scenario 56b3ef5f48342b9b (= features/templates/20) pins template-wide invariants but does not pin per-step CLI naming inside procedural subsections.

Router scope ruling: in scope of brief lead-231 items B+D. Invariant 1 of brief 001 ('shop-msg is sole inter-shop messaging surface; consumers never touch storage directly') is per-step discipline; a Constraints clause is too weak to prevent regression.

Vehicle: request_bugfix. Discriminator:
- Q1 (capability exists?): YES. Empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-architect.md line 272 and lead-po.md lines 160-162; the asymmetry exists exactly as the clarify describes. Lead-architect 'Sending a message' subsection lines 261-266 already names shop-msg read inbox explicitly, demonstrating the BC template's authors knew the explicit form — the omission in the Responding subsection is a gap, not a pattern.
- Q2 (pinned?): NO at the procedural-step granularity inside the Responding subsection.
- Q3 (behavioral or flat?): behavioral — tightens existing unpinned discipline. Behavioral → request_bugfix.

Carries: features/templates/46-lead-architect-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin (PO-authored; tightened beyond the BC's draft to add a third Then condition pinning that path-shape references like 'outbox/', '<BC root>/outbox/', 'the outbox file' also cannot appear without naming the CLI on the same step).

§4.4 closure signaling: when shopsystem-templates emits work_done on THIS work_id, that work_done implicitly sign-offs the original 9 scenarios dispatched under lead-bwu (per shop-system §4.4 loop closure shape). The BC should NOT emit a separate work_done on lead-bwu retroactively. The dispatch description names this expectation.

Implementer breadth expectation: scenario 46 pins the lead-architect 'Responding to a BC clarify' subsection, but the underlying invariant (per-step shop-msg read outbox naming wherever a procedural step reads outbox storage) is template-wide. Implementer should scan all four canonical templates for the asymmetry pattern, not just edit the cited subsection.
close_reason: Chain closure via lead-14e. Round 2 of §4.4 closure on lead-bwu — scenario 46 pinned per-step shop-msg read outbox in lead-architect 'Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' subsection. BC clarify on this round surfaced that scenario coverage was asymmetric (Implementer's fix landed across all four templates; coverage pinned only lead-architect). Round 3 (lead-14e) carried scenarios 47-49 to close the asymmetry; its terminal work_done implicitly signs off lead-e4g per dispatch description. Scenario 46 (work hash bd76... in features/) verified landed via lead-architect.md line 272 post-state.

title: Brief: shop bootstrap CLI surface (lead-shop + BC-shop scaffold over existing repo)
description: Brief 002 (in authoring): commits intent for a shop-bootstrap CLI surface — the user-facing way to take an existing repo and add the canonical shop scaffold (lead or BC). Building block for a later fully-automated product-setup workflow. Authoring driven by user-driver observation 2026-05-12: manual scaffolding does not scale across 5+ BC shops + ecommerce product staging.
notes:
2026-05-13 — lead-k8v clarify response (round 2 of brief-002 §4.4)

BC clarified that the 20 dispatched scenarios don't address bd init's
side effects (AGENTS.md, .claude/settings.json, CLAUDE.md beads-block,
.gitignore additions) and the bd-shim doesn't replicate them. User-driver
resolution: bootstrap invokes 'bd init --skip-agents' and shopsystem
templates reference bd discipline (bd prime etc.) as needed.

PO authored 4 additive scenarios (don't modify 26-45 — preserve hashes):
- 50: bootstrap invokes 'bd init --skip-agents' (tightens 32)
- 51: bootstrap output contains no AGENTS.md or .claude/settings.json
  (additive complement to 34)
- 52: canonical CLAUDE.md primer references bd discipline (bd prime
  + at least one bd-subcommand instruction)
- 53: bootstrap-written CLAUDE.md in target dir references bd discipline
  (parallels 45)

PO empirical verification: --skip-agents covers AGENTS.md, .claude/settings.json,
and (undocumented) the CLAUDE.md beads-block append. Only .gitignore
appendages (.dolt/, *.db, .beads-credential-key) remain — these are bd
runtime artifacts and per user decision should be preserved.

Architect's next move: dispatch additive request_bugfix carrying 50-53.
lead-k8v stays in_progress until terminal work_done.
close_reason: Brief 002 (shop bootstrap CLI surface) substantively met. All 5 scope items A-E pinned in shopsystem-templates via lead-k8v's 20 scenarios; round-2 side-effect resolution pinned via lead-2gr's 4 additive scenarios (50-53). Per brief-001 precedent: brief's committed intent is delivered; any further refinements would be follow-on briefs, not continuations of this one. Closing as feature complete.

title: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: B+D (CLI-only mailbox access + bd-decoupling) for brief lead-231
description:
Dispatch of items B and D from brief lead-231 (briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md) to the shopsystem-templates BC as one combined request_bugfix carrying all 9 PO-authored scenarios (features/templates/17..25-*.gherkin).

Parent intent: lead-231 (brief). Items A, C, E of the brief are complete; B+D close it.

Vehicle: request_bugfix per Architect discriminator. Pre-state empirically verified:
- shop-msg --help confirms pending {inbox,outbox} and read {inbox,outbox} are live top-level subcommands.
- shop-msg respond mechanism_observation --help confirms --provenance-ref is live; --bd-ref is gone.
- Canonical templates today direct readers to direct mailbox inspection (bc-implementer.md lines 10, 17, 24, 118-119 reference <BC root>/inbox/ and <BC root>/outbox/; bc-reviewer.md line 17 same; lead-po.md line 161 carries the explicit 'read the file directly during a dispatch' punt; lead-architect.md line 233 carries 'Inbox filename convention: <work_id>.yaml').
- bc-implementer.md lines 176-196 instruct 'bd create' as numbered step 1 before shop-msg respond mechanism_observation, with --bd-ref in step 2 (both removed by current CLI).
- shopsystem-templates CLAUDE.md 'BC inbox / outbox protocol' section names <work_id>.yaml and <work_id>-<response_type>.yaml filename conventions and the set-difference-on-filenames characterization of unprocessed.
- Behavior unpinned: none of features/templates/01..16 cover CLI-only or bd-decoupling discipline; this is finding-5 territory (behavior exists, no scenario pins it) → request_bugfix.

Bundling: combined (a) chosen over split (b) because both items rewrite the same six surfaces; in particular the D-scope 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section lives inside bc-implementer.md whose lines 10/17/24/118-119 are B-scope. Splitting would force two consecutive dispatches over the same file and risk the second undoing the first.

Sequencing: lead-kq0 is CLOSED (verified via bd show lead-kq0), satisfying the brief's sequencing constraint that B+D wait on the lead-kq0 (PDR-001) role-complete restructure landing first.
notes: 2026-05-12 (Architect subagent) — §4.4 clarify loop opened by this dispatch is being closed via child bead lead-e4g (request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates carrying scenario 46). Per the dispatch description on lead-e4g, the BC's work_done on lead-e4g implicitly closes the original 9 scenarios dispatched here as well — the BC will NOT emit a separate work_done on lead-bwu. Keep lead-bwu in_progress until lead-e4g lands work_done; at that point close both together. shop-msg send exit 0; inbox scenario hash e6bdf2f33bfae0d1.
close_reason: Chain closure via lead-14e. Round 1 (lead-bwu, 9 scenarios for items B+D) triggered BC clarify on asymmetry across 4 canonical templates. Round 2 (lead-e4g, scenario 46 for lead-architect 'Responding to BC clarify' subsection) triggered BC clarify on three sister subsections. Round 3 (lead-14e, scenarios 47-49 mirroring 46 across lead-po, bc-implementer, bc-reviewer) closed terminally with work_done complete. Per Architect's lead-14e dispatch, terminal work_done implicitly signs off the entire chain — the 9 original lead-bwu scenarios + scenario 46 + scenarios 47-49 are all landed in features/ with hashes matching. Item B (CLI-only mailbox access) and item D (bd-decoupling, --provenance-ref optional) substantively present across all 4 canonical templates per post-state spot-check.

title: Dispatch fix for lead-231.2 blocker: replace 4 fabricated @scenario_hash tags in shopsystem-messaging mechanism_observation features with canonical values

Reviewer blocked because the Implementer's side-effect claim of 'recomputed' @scenario_hash tags on 4 mechanism_observation feature files in the BC's own repo carried fabricated values. Dispatching request_maintenance (flat value-only fix, fully specified by Reviewer; no new scenarios). Non-dotted ID per lead-4wy. Parent (informally): lead-231. Reread BC outbox via 'shop-msg read outbox --bc-root .../shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-5cv' — status complete, scenario_hashes:[] (correct: request_maintenance carries none). Canonicals confirmed: respond_mechanism_observation.feature=950f7c76478e81c9; _collision=7c7a6d58fa71a60c; _path_separator=00f34c55c7ea9b93; _short_body=d2d860f1c41d98e9. Closes brief lead-231 item C (lead-231.2 substantive + lead-5cv tag fix) end-to-end.

description: Lead-side response to lead-231.2 work_done(blocked). Reviewer blocked because the Implementer's side-effect claim of 'recomputed' @scenario_hash tags on 4 mechanism_observation feature files in the BC's own repo carried fabricated values. title: Dispatch item C of brief lead-231: catalog schema bd-decoupling (6 message types) to shopsystem-messaging
description: request_bugfix dispatch to shopsystem-messaging covering item C of brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation). Bundles 6 scenarios from features/messaging/08..13-*.gherkin pinning the invariant 'minimal valid <type> can be constructed with no required bd-related field' for each of the 6 currently-implemented catalog schemas: AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, RequestMaintenance, Clarify, WorkDone, MechanismObservation. Pre-state empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/catalog/schemas.py: 5 of 6 schemas (RequestMaintenance, AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, Clarify, WorkDone) define NO bd_* field at all — their scenarios are regression pins against unpinned existing behavior. MechanismObservation.bd_ref is required (Field pattern '^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*-[a-z0-9]+$', line 178); its scenario tightens by demanding bd_ref become optional / removed / renamed to provenance_ref (Implementer's pick consistent with invariant). request_scenario_register and request_shop_card are deferred per the brief — no schemas exist for them in catalog/schemas.py. Bundled (not split) because the 6 scenarios share one invariant; the Implementer needs to see surface coherence. Parent brief: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md (lead-231).
close_reason: work_done(blocked) received. 6 assigned scenarios pass and substantive bd-decoupling work landed correctly (MechanismObservation.bd_ref -> optional provenance_ref). Reviewer terminated this loop on a concrete side-effect defect: 4 @scenario_hash tags in BC's own features/respond_mechanism_observation*.feature files carry fabricated values. Lead reverified empirically (cat file | scenarios hash matches Reviewer's canonicals; Implementer's claimed values present in files and wrong). Resolution path is a new fix-loop dispatched as lead-5cv (request_maintenance, value-only flat fix). This work_id is closed because the Reviewer terminated the loop — re-opening here would conflict with the §4.4 sealed-decision posture. lead-5cv carries live work going forward.

description: assign_scenarios dispatch to shopsystem-messaging covering item A of brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation). New capability => assign_scenarios. Parent brief: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md (lead-231).

title: Brief: inter-shop messaging encapsulation (shop-msg as sole surface; self-contained messages)
description: Lead-shop brief authored from mechanism_observation 'shopsystem-templates-kin' against lead-kq0. Names two invariants (shop-msg is the sole inter-shop messaging surface; inter-shop messages are self-contained), the bd-decoupling corollary, and five scope items (A: shop-msg CLI surface; B: templates rewrite; C: catalog schema decoupling from bd; D: template-language decoupling from bd; E: spec edits to sections 4 and 5). Sequencing: A+C+E parallel; B+D wait on lead-kq0 (PDR-001) closing. Scenario authoring deferred until Architect verifies BC pre-state and picks vehicles. Artifact: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md.
notes:
2026-05-12 (PO subagent, brief 001 items B+D, round 3 follow-up) — BC clarify on lead-e4g (request_bugfix carrying scenario 46) confirmed the regression vulnerability is present in three sister subsections that scenario 46 does not pin: lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' (line 158), bc-implementer '## Your job' (line 26), bc-reviewer '## What you read' (line 15). The Implementer's textual FIX extended across all four templates correctly, but coverage only pinned one. Without parallel scenarios, future edits to the other three subsections could regress to bare-verb 'Read the inbox YAML' (or similar) and only the template-wide encapsulation scenarios (6adb97ba0d0498c5, ff97230bf1e36a81, f279d66b8313e3fc) would gate them — and the BC's clarify text correctly notes those are too weak for per-step discipline (a bare 'Read' step coexists with 'shop-msg read inbox' elsewhere in the same template, satisfying template-wide substring presence while leaving the procedural step ambiguous).

Scope decision (router): option (a) accepted — three parallel scenarios mirroring 46's shape. Not (b) (BC's textual fix needs scenario coverage to be load-bearing). Not (c) (tightening the template-wide encapsulation scenarios with per-step granularity would lose the presence/absence checks they currently make; per-subsection scenarios are the cleaner shape). Outline-form consolidation deferred — scenario 46 is already dispatched + hashed; a brief revision can fold all four into a Scenario Outline if the pattern continues to recur.

Authored: features/templates/47-lead-po-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/48-bc-implementer-your-job-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/49-bc-reviewer-what-you-read-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin. Each mirrors scenario 46 exactly: locate the subsection by heading boundary (## or ### until next heading), three Then conditions — (i) every step whose action is reading the mailbox names the literal CLI substring on the same step, (ii) no bare-verb regression mode, (iii) no path-shape regression mode. Vary only by template name, subsection heading text, and target substring (read outbox vs. read inbox).

Pre-state verification of BC's sketch: subsection headings as sketched (verified by grep). Two asymmetries noted but kept out of scope of this dispatch:
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 1 (line 28) names BOTH 'shop-msg read inbox' (reading) AND 'shop-msg pending inbox' (listing). The new scenario 48 pins only the read-inbox action, parallel to scenario 46's narrow target. The pending-inbox-listing action is already covered template-wide by scenarios 17/18 (uses-shop-msg-cli-for-mailbox-access). Same shape applies to bc-reviewer 'What you read' step 1 (lines 17-20) — scenario 49 pins only read-inbox.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' is H2 (## Your job); lead-po's target is H3 (### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI); bc-reviewer's target is H2 (## What you read); lead-architect's target (scenario 46) is H3. Scenario 46's locator clause 'ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###)' handles both depths uniformly; new scenarios reuse the same clause verbatim. Consistent.

Round-4 outlook (flag): With round 3 closing the four-template per-subsection-CLI-naming regression mode, the remaining brief-001 B+D regression-mode surface is:
- Procedural CLI naming for the OTHER mailbox actions inside the same/adjacent procedural subsections. Specifically: writing the outbox response. lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify'; lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4 cites the same. But no scenario currently pins per-step CLI naming for the RESPOND action. Same regression mode: a future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only the template-wide scenarios would gate it. If round 4 is desired, the same Scenario-Outline-amenable shape applies across all four templates' write-outbox / write-inbox actions.
- Procedural CLI naming for 'shop-msg respond work_done' (Implementer/Reviewer) and 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' (Implementer). bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 names 'shop-msg respond work_done'; 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section names 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation'; bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' names both 'shop-msg respond work_done' and 'shop-msg respond clarify'. None of these are pinned by per-step scenarios.

Recommendation: defer round 4 to a deliberate brief revision rather than chasing it dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline — and the cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly. Until then, the four parallel scenarios are correct and load-bearing.

Did NOT dispatch. Did NOT compose shop-msg send. Architect opens a request_bugfix dispatch bead and sends the three new scenarios as additive to lead-e4g's coverage. (Open question for Architect: dispatch as a separate work_id, or amend lead-e4g? The BC's clarify is open against lead-e4g; the cleanest closure is one new request_bugfix work_id whose description references lead-e4g's clarify as the trigger.)
close_reason:
Brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation) substantively complete. All 5 scope items landed:
- A (shop-msg CLI surface — pending enumeration, read inbox, --bc filter): closed via lead-231.1 (shopsystem-messaging).
- B (templates rewrite — CLI-only mailbox access): closed via lead-bwu chain (lead-bwu -> lead-e4g -> lead-14e) — 9 + 1 + 3 = 13 scenarios landed in shopsystem-templates features/ pinning per-step CLI naming for READ-mailbox actions across all four canonical templates (lead-po, lead-architect, bc-implementer, bc-reviewer). Post-state spot-check confirms substantive landing.
- C (catalog schema bd-decoupling): closed via lead-231.2 + lead-231.3 (shopsystem-messaging).
- D (template-language decoupling from bd): closed via lead-bwu chain. --provenance-ref optional, --bd-ref removed, no 'create a bead first' precondition language in any of the four canonical templates per post-state spot-check.
- E (spec edits §4/§5): closed.

Round-4 outlook (per-step pinning for WRITE-mailbox actions: shop-msg respond clarify | work_done | mechanism_observation) opened as lead-6t3 — follow-on refinement that emerged from the §4.4 process, not original brief 001 scope. Deferred to deliberate brief revision per recommendation in notes; a Scenario Outline can supersede 46-49 cleanly when authored.

Scenario register for shopsystem-templates not yet authored (per lead-otu); noted, no action this close.

Brief 002 (lead-3d7) was held pending lead-bwu landing — now unblocked, dispatch is a separate next step.

title: Product venv installs drift from BC source after BC work lands (no auto-reinstall)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-79q dispatch.

The product venv at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/ has shop_msg installed as a regular package (not editable). When BCs update their source (lead-231.1 added 'pending' subcommand; lead-bwu/14e/ijr updated CLI behavior), the venv install does NOT update automatically. Lead-shop CLI invocations against shop-msg (and likely shop-templates similarly) see the OLDER installed surface, not the BC's live source.

Concrete impact today: shop-msg pending didn't exist in the venv install despite being present in BC source. Architect for lead-79q hit this and worked around by direct ls of inbox/outbox — a violation of brief 001 invariant 1 (encapsulation; CLI is sole messaging surface) forced by tooling staleness. Re-installed manually via 'pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging' which fixed it. shop-templates likely has the same issue (Architect for lead-79q also noted product venv shop-templates lacks bootstrap/update).

Two facets:
(a) Install mode: BCs should be installed editable (-e) so source changes flow without reinstall. Currently appears to be non-editable.
(b) Reinstall trigger: even if editable, fresh BC clones / version changes need a way to refresh. SessionStart hook in brief 003 could include 'pip install -e repos/*/' to keep the venv synced.

Adjacent / related:
- lead-zmi (shop-msg PATH ergonomics) — same theme, different facet.
- Brief 003 SessionStart hook design — should include venv-resync as part of activating each shop.

The 'ls workaround' the Architect used is a discipline concern: the lead-architect template's encapsulation guidance should be tightened to say 'if shop-msg pending fails, FIX THE TOOLING (reinstall/PATH/version) not bypass'. Worth a separate template-tightening bead if recurring.

title: bc-implementer template should require hash-recompute verification after @scenario_hash tag edits (drain of messaging-d64)
description:
Drain of messaging-d64 mechanism_observation from shopsystem-messaging, observed during lead-5cv.

Finding: bc-implementer canonical template does not require the Implementer to verify a @scenario_hash:<value> tag by piping the file through 'scenarios hash' before claiming the tag is correct. In lead-231.2 the Implementer wrote 4 fabricated hash values; Reviewer caught it but only via lead-side reconciliation cost (full §4.4 round-trip via lead-5cv).

BC's proposed action: Add a 'verify hash tags' step in the bc-implementer template's 'Doing the work' sections for assign_scenarios and request_bugfix (and an equivalent clause for request_maintenance when it touches scenario tags) requiring 'scenarios hash' recompute equality before terminal response.

Target BC: shopsystem-templates (the canonical bc-implementer template is the templates BC's product; messaging BC cannot self-fix).

Vehicle hint: request_bugfix (template-language tightening of unpinned existing behavior).

Natural fold-in with lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook for brief 001 — per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions). Both items tighten the bc-implementer canonical template via additional procedural discipline; both could be carried by one brief revision that consolidates 46-49 + write-mailbox cases + hash-verify step into a coordinated dispatch. Worth considering together when the next brief revision is authored.

Also adjacent: lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw vs wire form). Tightening hash-verify discipline in the template is load-bearing; ensuring the BC's bc-implementer knows WHICH form to verify against requires lead-wgv to be resolved first (or the discipline step to be explicit about it).

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook), lead-wgv (canonicalization), lead-5cv (original incident).

title: Round-4 outlook: per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions (shop-msg respond clarify | work_done | mechanism_observation) across all four canonical templates
description:
Follow-on refinement that emerged from brief 001 items B+D §4.4 process (rounds 2-3 dispatch chain lead-bwu -> lead-e4g -> lead-14e closed; scenarios 46-49 landed pinning per-step CLI naming for READ-mailbox actions across all four canonical templates).

Remaining regression surface flagged in lead-231 round-4 outlook notes:

- WRITING outbox responses: lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify' but no scenario pins per-step CLI naming for the respond action. Same regression mode as scenarios 46-49: future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only template-wide scenarios would gate it.
- Same applies to lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 ('shop-msg respond work_done') and 'Surfacing mechanism observations' ('shop-msg respond mechanism_observation') — not pinned per-step.
- bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' ('shop-msg respond work_done' / 'shop-msg respond clarify') — not pinned per-step.

Recommendation from lead-231 notes: defer to a deliberate brief revision rather than dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline. Cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly.

Vehicle when dispatched: likely request_bugfix (behavior exists in templates, no scenario pins it at per-step granularity — finding-5 territory). PO authors a Scenario Outline; Architect verifies BC pre-state empirically (read each respond-step in each template, confirm CLI is named) and dispatches.

Not blocking anything. Brief 001 (lead-231) is being closed as substantively complete; this is a separate follow-on concern.

title: scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during lead-e4g dispatch (request_bugfix closing lead-bwu §4.4 loop).

The Architect observed: 'scenarios hash < features/templates/46-*.gherkin' (against the raw scenario body) produces 06328160e83d2995, while the hash 'shop-msg send' persists on the wire (validated by 'shop-msg read') is e6bdf2f33bfae0d1. The CLI computes against a wrapped+sentinel-tagged form, not the raw body.

This is the Slice-16-shaped mismatch named in the architect's role contract anti-rationalization section. Same shape almost surfaced in lead-231.2 / lead-5cv (Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values; Reviewer caught via 'cat file | scenarios hash' which DID match the post-edit canonical) — but that case's reconciliation worked because both Implementer and Reviewer used raw-body hashing on already-tagged feature files, where the @scenario_hash tag had been hand-edited. The two-canonicalization-domains problem didn't bite because both parties stayed within one domain.

For lead-e4g and any future dispatch, the canonical question matters:
- The BC's ScenarioPayload.hash == compute_scenario_hash(gherkin) enforcement uses which canonicalization?
- The 'scenarios hash' CLI computes which form?
- Implementers verifying tags need to match against what the catalog enforces.

Three resolution shapes:
(a) One canonical form across both. 'scenarios hash' wraps its input the same way shop-msg does; tag values across feature files / inboxes / outboxes are byte-identical for identical scenario semantic content.
(b) Two named forms with clear scope. 'scenarios hash --raw' and 'scenarios hash --wire' (or similar); documented when each applies.
(c) Status quo + documentation. Names the two domains and where each is canonical; templates and procedural reminders cite the right form.

(a) is the cleanest invariant; (b) is most explicit; (c) is lowest-touch but most failure-prone.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap), lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption) — same shape of latent protocol/canonicalization constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Adjacent: lead-5cv's Implementer-defect-and-Reviewer-catch worked because both used raw-body hashing on already-tagged files (within one canonicalization domain). A future dispatch where one party uses the wire-form hash and the other uses raw-body would silently disagree.

Concrete impact for current work: lead-e4g's @scenario_hash tag in the BC inbox is the wire form. The BC's Implementer / Reviewer must validate against the wire form, not via raw-body 'scenarios hash'. Worth a procedural reminder in the dispatch description (already included by Architect for lead-e4g).

title: work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).

title: Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept
description:
Surfaced by Architect during brief-001 items A+C dispatch on 2026-05-12.

During dispatch of lead-231.1 (AssignScenarios) and lead-231.2 (RequestBugfix) to shopsystem-messaging, the Architect used dotted child bead IDs (lead-231.1, lead-231.2) as work_ids. Both schemas accepted them — neither AssignScenarios.work_id nor RequestBugfix.work_id carries a pattern constraint (just str). But Clarify.work_id has pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which rejects dots.

This is a latent failure: if the BC sends Clarify on either lead-231.1 or lead-231.2, the BC-side 'shop-msg respond clarify --work-id lead-231.1' will fail schema validation. The protocol's symmetry across message types breaks at clarify-time.

What to do: align work_id patterns across catalog schemas. Three shapes worth considering — (a) tighten AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix/RequestMaintenance/WorkDone/MechanismObservation to share Clarify's pattern, constraining work_id naming uniformly; (b) loosen Clarify to match the others (just str), accepting more chaos but unblocking; (c) pick a shared canonical pattern (probably (a)'s shape with an explicit grammar) and apply uniformly with a single Pydantic type alias. Architect's call after pre-state verification.

Adjacent finding (do not scope here, just note): shop-msg has no recall command for in-flight dispatches, and the encapsulation invariant from lead-231 prohibits rm-ing inbox files. The two existing lead-231.1/.2 dispatches will tolerate this gap because they're more likely to round-trip via work_done than clarify, but recall-side encapsulation is a structural concern worth its own future brief.

Related: lead-231 (brief that surfaced this).

title: Restructure lead-po and lead-architect templates to be role-complete (PDR-001)
description:
Lead-shop work item tracking the dispatch to shopsystem-templates BC for the
role-complete restructure of lead-po.md and lead-architect.md.

Intent driver: PDR-001 (pdr/001-role-templates-role-complete.md).

The PO subagent authors scenarios in this repo's features/templates/ pinning
what role-completeness means against §3.2's activity catalogue. The Architect
subagent then verifies the templates BC's pre-state empirically, applies the
message-type discriminator, and dispatches via shop-msg send to the
shopsystem-templates BC.

This beads ID is the work_id that flows outward in the inter-shop message
per §6.
notes:
Architect dispatched assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates BC on 2026-05-12.

Empirical pre-state verification: ran `shop-templates show lead-po` and
`shop-templates show lead-architect`. Confirmed: neither template names the
§3.2 activities the role-complete restructure requires (Interview stakeholder,
Maintain product brief, Write PDR, Reconcile scenario registers, Write ADRs,
Maintain structurizr, BC decomposition). The capability genuinely does not
exist today — discriminator Q1 → assign_scenarios.

Scenarios 09-16 all passed well-formed Gherkin sufficiency check. CLI computed
hashes and added @bc/@scenario_hash tags. Message deposited at
repos/shopsystem-templates/inbox/lead-kq0.yaml.

Scenario hashes (CLI-computed):
- 09 f1ac9534c1d58318
- 10 dddd6c3b2eed7e45
- 11 9a9421ad59ee5d67
- 12 e522f7393dfcd1c1
- 13 a481db51463526d2
- 14 0aea22a97e63d4f8
- 15 5ccb3fb1122f9341
- 16 a6b3e510821aba52

PO's 4 open questions accepted as-is by Architect with rationale (BC has no
existing 'guidance pending' convention; depth-3+ is a meaningful step; both
short and full §3.2 phrasing appear across scenarios; PDR-001 permits
guidance-pending marker for activities lacking sufficiency-check shape).

Awaiting work_done from BC. Close lead-kq0 on reconciliation.
close_reason: work_done received via shop-msg read outbox (status: complete). All 8 scenario hashes match dispatch one-to-one (f1ac9534c1d58318, dddd6c3b2eed7e45, 9a9421ad59ee5d67, e522f7393dfcd1c1, a481db51463526d2, 0aea22a97e63d4f8, 5ccb3fb1122f9341, a6b3e510821aba52). Post-state spot-check via shop-templates show lead-po / show lead-architect confirms substantive restructure: all 5 PO + 8 Architect §3.2 activities now present as ### subsections with one-sentence-plus guidance, where at dispatch they were absent. BC reports BDD 53/0 and probes considered+dismissed. No scenario register exists under features/templates/ yet (raw .gherkin files only); no ad-hoc register created per scope.

title: request_bugfix dispatched: shop-templates bootstrap+update reject missing --target
description: Dispatch of lead-03r consistency-pass to shopsystem-templates BC. Two adjacent missing-required-arg gaps tightened: bootstrap missing --target (TypeError at cli.py:272 Path(None)) and update missing --target (TypeError at cli.py:322 Path(None)). Scenarios 55 and 56 in features/templates/. Precedent: lead-ijr added _validate_shop_name at cli.py:232-250 mirroring _validate_shop_type; Implementer will likely add _validate_target the same shape.
close_reason: work_done complete from shopsystem-templates BC: 108 passed/0 failed; 2 dispatched scenario hashes (fe59a11a88a9ab60 bootstrap, 8fe363bd46cb766c update) match the dispatch; 5 regression-attested pins re-verified. Post-state spot-check confirmed: _validate_target helper added at cli.py:253 paralleling _validate_shop_name; _cmd_bootstrap (line 301) and _cmd_update (line 361) invoke it before any Path() construction. Re-ran both failing invocations: each exits 2 cleanly with stderr naming --target and no traceback. Reviewer's adversarial probes (mutate _validate_target to no-op, mutate diagnostic to omit --target, byte+mtime snapshot of pre-existing scaffold) all confirmed teeth. Monitor pattern milestone: work_done delivered via real-time event notification before any polling.

title: shop-templates bootstrap/update missing --target raises TypeError (consistency pass adjacent to lead-1uo)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 by lead-1uo PO + Architect during scenario 54 authoring/dispatch.

Two adjacent missing-required-arg gaps in shop-templates CLI, same shape as lead-1uo (which only covers --shop-name on bootstrap):

1. shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name foo (missing --target)
   → TypeError at cli.py:242 (Path(args.target) with None).

2. shop-templates update --shop-type bc (missing --target)
   → TypeError at cli.py:292 (Path(args.target) with None).

Note: shop-templates update missing --shop-type already produces a clean diagnostic (_validate_shop_type catches it at lines 394-406). So this consistency pass is narrower than 'audit all subcommands' — only --target on bootstrap + update need tightening.

Vehicle: request_bugfix (same shape as lead-ijr currently in flight). Could fold with lead-ijr's fix if its Implementer's solution generalizes (uniform _validate-style missing-arg diagnostic across CLI); otherwise dispatch separately after lead-ijr round-trip surfaces the chosen pattern.

Defer dispatch until lead-ijr (the lead-1uo fix) returns work_done — then decide whether the Implementer's fix shape extends naturally or whether a separate dispatch is cleaner.

Related: lead-1uo (--shop-name parent), lead-ijr (in-flight fix dispatch).
close_reason: Consistency-pass closure. Both gaps tightened: (1) shop-templates bootstrap missing --target now exits 2 cleanly with stderr naming --target (scenario fe59a11a88a9ab60); (2) shop-templates update missing --target same shape (scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c). Dispatched as lead-79q request_bugfix; Implementer added _validate_target helper paralleling _validate_shop_name from lead-ijr. work_done complete from BC. With this closure, brief 002's bootstrap surface has all known missing-required-arg gaps pinned with clean-error discipline (--shop-type, --shop-name, --target across bootstrap+update). Reviewer flagged that empty-string and nonexistent-path cases for --target are out-of-scope and consistent with --shop-name precedent (same shape, not gaps).

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: PO authored scenarios 55 and 56 (parallel scenarios, not Scenario Outline). Scenario 55: bootstrap rejects missing --target with argparse-style error; mirrors scenario 54 (--shop-name) shape — exit 2, stderr names --target, no traceback / no TypeError, witness target dir untouched (no .claude/agents, no .beads, no CLAUDE.md, no .gitignore). Scenario 56: update rejects missing --target; same exit-2 + named-arg + no-traceback discipline, but the negation pins 'pre-existing shop scaffold is unchanged' (byte+mtime snapshot of .claude/agents/, plus CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/ unchanged), reflecting that update operates on an existing scaffold rather than creating one. Rationale for parallel-over-outline: (1) scenario 54 is flat, parallel preserves symmetry; (2) the negations meaningfully diverge — bootstrap asserts nothing-created against an empty dir, update asserts nothing-modified against an existing scaffold; (3) per-row readability matters for future debugging. Files: features/templates/55-bootstrap-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error.gherkin, features/templates/56-update-rejects-missing-target-with-argparse-error.gherkin. Tags (@scenario_hash + @bc:shopsystem-templates) deferred to Architect at dispatch. Pre-state confirmed in cli.py: bootstrap_cmd / update_cmd both declare --target with default=None and no required=True (lines 442-446 and 453-457); _cmd_bootstrap at line 272 and _cmd_update at line 322 both call Path(args.target) which raises TypeError on None. Implementation pattern likely mirrors _validate_shop_name (cli.py:232-250): add _validate_target helper invoked early in each _cmd_*, before any Path() construction. No further missing-required-arg gaps known in shop-templates CLI after these scenarios land.

title: bootstrap-rejects-missing-shop-name-with-argparse-error (dispatch of scenario 54 to shopsystem-templates)
description: Dispatch child of lead-1uo (parent P3 bug). Scope: scenario 54 only (tight scope; Monitor-pattern test dispatch). Tightening of unpinned existing behavior — bootstrap currently raises TypeError at cli.py:167 when --shop-name omitted; scenario 54 pins exit 2, argparse-shaped stderr naming --shop-name, no traceback, no scaffold written. Empirically verified by Architect 2026-05-13 via /tmp/probe-lead-1uo probe (TypeError reproduced). Adjacent same-shape gaps (missing --target on bootstrap; missing --target on update) deliberately NOT folded — separate P3 beads to follow. Parent: lead-1uo. Related scenario file: features/templates/54-bootstrap-rejects-missing-shop-name-with-argparse-error.gherkin.
close_reason: work_done from shopsystem-templates verified: status complete, scenario hash 3c8612d20608e9a3 matches dispatch. Post-state spot-check: _validate_shop_name exists at cli.py:232 with the diagnostic shape scenario 54 pins; _cmd_bootstrap invokes it at cli.py:268 returning exit 2 before any scaffold side effects. Empirical re-probe: 'shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --target /tmp/probe-reconcile-ijr' (omitting --shop-name) exits 2, stderr names --shop-name, no traceback, target directory remains empty. 106 BDD passing (was 105+1). No regression on adjacent scenarios (d6e935bca62d0039, 7941cdc591cd2c3b, 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, 32d99f6d4a2dad37). Adjacent same-shape gap on --target (cli.py:272) deferred to lead-03r per separate dispatch.

title: shop-templates bootstrap raises TypeError when --shop-name omitted (should be argparse error or default-derived)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during brief-002 reconciliation by Architect. Empirical probe ran 'shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --target .' (without --shop-name) and got TypeError (None passed to str.replace) instead of an argparse-style error.

Not a regression introduced by brief-002 work — pre-existing surface gap. Either:
(a) --shop-name should be argparse-required, raising a clean parse error.
(b) --shop-name should default-derive (e.g., from --target basename).

P3 — ergonomic, not blocking. Adjacent to brief-002's CLI surface but out of scope of its committed invariants. Surfaced during post-state probe; not part of any in-flight dispatch.

Related: lead-3d7 (brief 002 — closed).
notes: Scenario 54 authored by lead-po (PDR-002 path-a subagent) 2026-05-13. File: features/templates/54-bootstrap-rejects-missing-shop-name-with-argparse-error.gherkin. Realization pinned: --shop-name argparse-required; bootstrap exits 2 with argparse-shaped error naming --shop-name; stderr contains no TypeError / traceback; no scaffold written. Tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates at dispatch by Architect. Adjacent gaps spotted (NOT folded in, flagged for Architect): bootstrap without --target raises TypeError; update without --target raises TypeError. Same shape (None reaches Path() or str.replace() instead of clean argparse-style error). Architect may decide whether to expand scope of dispatch or open separate beads.
close_reason: Parent intent delivered. Child dispatch lead-ijr closed: scenario 54 (3c8612d20608e9a3) pinned and implemented in shopsystem-templates. Realization: --shop-name validated manually with argparse-shaped diagnostic (option (a) from the issue). Post-state empirically verified — exit 2, named flag in stderr, no traceback, no scaffold side effects. Adjacent same-shape gaps on --target (bootstrap and update) flagged in NOTES and tracked separately under lead-03r.

title: bc-implementer singleton-inbox presumption constrains lead-shop dispatch cadence
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during brief-002 Architect pre-state verification.

The bc-implementer canonical template at repos/shopsystem-templates/.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md line 49 says verbatim 'There is exactly one file there.' The Implementer subagent's role contract presumes a singleton inbox. The bc-router CLAUDE.md does not enforce serialization either — its dispatch rule is 'any unprocessed inbox dispatches the Implementer' with no ordering guarantee.

Concrete consequence: the lead shop cannot dispatch a new message to a BC while another work_id is still in flight (still in the BC's inbox, not yet processed to outbox). Architect for brief-002 detected this and held dispatch because lead-bwu was still pending in the templates BC inbox.

Design question: should the protocol/template
- (a) explicitly enforce serial in-flight (one message at a time per BC; lead shop blocks on outbox before next send), formalizing the current presumption?
- (b) relax the singleton presumption (Implementer template handles N-message inbox; BC-router picks one to dispatch per turn)?
- (c) introduce a queue concept in shop-msg (lead can deposit multiple; BC processes in FIFO order; protocol exposes 'next' subcommand)?

(a) is the lowest-touch and matches current template behavior. (c) is the most flexible and may align with the brief-001 invariant (shop-msg encapsulates storage). (b) is a middle ground.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done(blocked) protocol gap) — same shape of latent protocol constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Currently lead-bwu remains pending in templates BC inbox; brief-002 dispatch is held until it lands. This bead captures the structural concern, not the operational hold.

title: shop-msg not on default PATH — requires venv activation for cold dispatches
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

shop-msg is installed at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg but the venv is not activated by default. Any agent dispatched cold (fresh subagent, new shell) must 'source .venv/bin/activate' first or invoke the absolute path; otherwise 'shop-msg ...' fails with command-not-found. The lead-architect's reconciliation pass hit this and worked around it.

What needs to be done: pick one of (a) ensure venv activation is automatic for any lead-shop session (devcontainer postCreate / shell rc), (b) install shop-msg outside the venv so it is on PATH unconditionally, (c) update subagent templates and CLAUDE.md to always invoke the absolute path or activate first. (c) is the lowest-touch / no install changes; (a) or (b) are more systemic. Choose during pickup.

title: Scenario register surface for templates BC (lead-architect activity currently degenerate)
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

The post-lead-kq0 lead-architect template names 'Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work' as a §3.2 activity. No register surface exists under features/templates/ — only the raw .gherkin files (01-16). The activity is structurally degenerate for the templates BC until a register is introduced; the role names a responsibility the mechanism doesn't yet realize.

What needs to be done: decide between (a) introduce a per-BC scenario register surface in the lead shop carrying authored / dispatched / signed-off status (designed once for all BCs, not just templates), or (b) accept reconciliation against the templates BC as degenerate by design and document why. If (a), the design likely interacts with the lead-shop drain workflow noted as deferred in findings/from-mechanism-observation-v1.md §6.
